metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5072
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1: Apache (aka. KIAA1107, gene BTBD8) protein expression increases during development, and co-localizes with synaptotagmin-1 (SYT1) in immunofluorescence confocal imaging of DIV17 primary cortical neurons.

Figure 2: FLAG-tagged pulldowns for Apache/KIAA1107 and mass-spec showed AP2A1, AP2B1 (and other endocytotic proteins) as interactors, westernblots confirmed the reverse-IP in mouse brain extracts using a monoclonal antibody against AP2A1.

Figure 3a,b: biochemical fractions showed;
"KIAA1107 was significantly enriched in the CCV fraction, although to a lesser extent than clathrin, and could be stripped by treatment with Tris buffer (Figure 3A). These data show that KIAA1107 is a protein associated with the coat component and not with the vesicle fraction."

KIAA1107 was "co-enriched with AP2 in the nerve-terminal-derived fractions LS1 and LP2, containing SVs and endosomal membranes, and its distribution roughly paralleled that of AP2 in other fractions (Figure 3B)."

Figure 3c: endogenous Apache/KIAA1107 co-localized with AP2-alpha (AP2A1) and dynamin1(DNM1) in immunofluorescence confocal imaging of DIV17 primary cortical neurons.

authors conclude: "These data suggest that KIAA1107 is closely associated with intracellular vesicular structures and binds specifically to AP2 on CCVs.". Experimental description: species used in confocal imaging:
"We evaluated Kiaa1107 mRNA and protein levels in various tissues and brain areas of adult mice and determined its developmental expression profile in the intact mouse cortex and primary neuronal cultures (Figures 1A-1F)."

on the protein identifier:
"Here, we characterize the highly conserved AP2-interacting clathrin-endocytosis protein APache (NP_001007575.2) and investigate its physiological role in neuronal development and synaptic function."

NCBI accession NP_001007575 matches uniprot accession D3YUB6 (gene names Btbd8, Gm16115, Kiaa1107)
